meta.focus: Review of Kanji, Vocabulary, and Grammar from Lessons 5–6.

sections.title: Part 1: Conversation Check
sections.instructions: Read each conversation and select the best answer.

items.title: Conversation 1
items.context: Rikizo is buying ingredients at a supermarket to cook dinner at home.

lines.jp: すみません。野菜と 牛肉は どこに ありますか。
野菜は そこに あります。牛肉は あそこです。
ありがとうございます。今晩、牛肉と 野菜で 料理を 作りたいです。
いい ですね。ここの 食べ物は とても おいしいですよ。どうぞ。

items.question: What does Rikizo want to do tonight?
choices: Make a dish with beef and vegetables
Go food shopping with a friend
Drink green tea and buy food
items.answer: Make a dish with beef and vegetables
items.explanation: A says 今晩、牛肉と野菜で料理を作りたいです — 'Tonight I want to make a dish with beef and vegetables.' で after ingredients marks them as the means or material used for cooking. 作りたいです = 作る + たいです (want to make).

items.title: Conversation 2
items.context: Rikizo and Yamakawa are deciding where to go for lunch during their lunch break at the office.

lines.jp: りきぞさん、昼休みに いっしょに 昼ご飯を 食べに 行きましょう。
いい ですね。今日は 鳥肉の 料理が 食べたいです。
駅の 前の レストランは 牛肉より 鳥肉の 味が おいしいですよ。
わかりました。そのレストランで 昼ご飯を 食べましょう。

items.question: Why does A recommend the restaurant in front of the station?
choices: The chicken there tastes better than beef
It is the closest restaurant to the office
It serves the same food as the supermarket
Rikizo has never eaten beef before
items.answer: The chicken there tastes better than beef
items.explanation: A says 駅の前のレストランは牛肉より鳥肉の味がおいしいですよ — 'At the restaurant in front of the station, the chicken tastes more delicious than beef.' より marks the lesser item in a comparison: 牛肉より (more than beef) → chicken is the winner.

items.title: Conversation 3 (Casual)
items.context: Two close friends chat after eating lunch together, comparing food preferences.

lines.jp: 今日の 昼ご飯、おいしかった？
うん、鳥肉の 料理が とても おいしかったよ。
私も 牛肉より 鳥肉が 好きだよ。
同じだね。つぎも いっしょに 食べよう。

items.question: What does A say about chicken versus beef?
choices: A likes chicken more than beef
A thinks beef and chicken taste the same
A has never eaten chicken before
A wants to cook chicken at home
items.answer: A likes chicken more than beef
items.explanation: A says 私も牛肉より鳥肉が好きだよ — 'I also like chicken more than beef.' より is the comparison particle: 牛肉より (compared to beef) means chicken is preferred. 好きだよ is the casual plain-form predicate of 好き (to like).

sections.title: Part 2: Grammar Scramble
sections.instructions: Construct the sentence by clicking the words in the correct order.

items.q: Tonight Rikizo wants to make a beef and vegetable dish.
items.explanation: りきぞ + は (topic) + 今晩 + 牛肉と (beef and ~) + 野菜で (using vegetables — で marks the ingredient or means) + 料理を (cuisine as object) + 作りたいです (want to make). 今晩 (tonight) can naturally float sentence-initially. が marks a subject with contrast, not a topic. の links nouns but cannot connect to で here. 食べたいです means 'want to eat,' not 'want to make.'

items.q: Chicken is more delicious than beef.
items.explanation: 鳥肉 + は (topic) + 牛肉 + より (than — comparison particle marking the lesser item) + おいしいです. The pattern 'A は B より adjective' means 'A is more adjective than B.' より attaches to B, the standard of comparison. まで means 'until/as far as.' ほど is used in the reverse pattern: B ほど adjective ない = 'not as adjective as B.'

items.q: During the lunch break I ate lunch with Yamakawa.
items.explanation: 昼休みに (during the lunch break — に marks the time period) + やまかわ + と (together with — と marks a companion for shared actions) + 昼ご飯を (lunch as object) + 食べました. で after a person is wrong — と is the companion particle. が marks やまかわ as the subject, not a companion. 飲みました means 'drank' — you eat (食べる) a meal, not drink it.

items.q: This food and that drink have the same taste.
items.explanation: この (this) + 食べ物と (food and ~) + その (that) + 飲み物は (drink as topic) + 同じ (same — directly precedes nouns WITHOUT な) + 味です. 同じ is the main exception among na-adjectives: you say 同じ味, not 同じな味. 別の means 'another/different' — the opposite of same. 買い物は (shopping as topic) doesn't fit the food-taste context.

items.q: I want to buy food and drinks at the store.
items.explanation: 店で (at the store — で marks location of an action) + 食べ物と (food and ~) + 飲み物を (drinks as object) + 買いたいです (want to buy). に after 店 marks it as a destination of movement (行く, 来る) not a place of action. から means 'from.' 作りたいです means 'want to make,' not 'want to buy.'

sections.title: Part 3: Reading — Shopping and Cooking
sections.instructions: Read the passage and answer the comprehension questions.

passage.jp: 私は 毎日 スーパーで 買い物を します。
passage.en: I go shopping at the supermarket every day.

passage.jp: きのう、野菜と 牛肉を 買いました。
passage.en: Yesterday I bought vegetables and beef.

passage.jp: 家で 牛肉と 野菜の 料理を 作りました。
passage.en: I made a beef and vegetable dish at home.

passage.jp: 料理の 味が とても よかったです。
passage.en: The taste of the dish was very good.

passage.jp: 今日の 昼休みに、鳥肉の 料理も 食べました。
passage.en: During today's lunch break, I also ate a chicken dish.

passage.jp: 鳥肉の 料理は 牛肉より おいしかったです。
passage.en: The chicken dish was more delicious than beef.

questions.q: 私は きのう 何を 買いましたか。
questions.explanation: 私はきのう野菜と牛肉を買いました — 'Yesterday I bought vegetables and beef.' The answer is stated directly in sentence 2 of the passage.

questions.q: 料理の 味は どうでしたか。
questions.a: とても よかったです
choices: とても よかったです
あまり よくなかったです
牛肉より よかったです
鳥肉より まずかったです
questions.explanation: 料理の味がとても よかったです — 'The taste of the dish was very good.' よかったです is the past polite form of いい: the irregular い→よ stem change gives よかった + です. Note: いかったです is a common mistake but is incorrect.

questions.q: 鳥肉の 料理と 牛肉の 料理では、どちらが おいしかったですか。
choices: どちらも おいしかった
どちらも おいしくなかった
questions.explanation: 鳥肉の料理は牛肉よりおいしかったです — 'The chicken dish was more delicious than beef.' より marks 牛肉 as the lesser comparison point, so 鳥肉の料理 is the more delicious option.

sections.title: Part 4: Kanji Readings
sections.instructions: Select the correct reading for the [bracketed] word.

items.q: [牛肉]で 料理を 作ります。
items.explanation: 牛肉 (beef) is read as gyuuniku. 牛 (N4.5, cow) on-reading ぎゅう + 肉 (N4.5, meat) reading にく = ぎゅうにく. Contrast with 鳥肉 (とりにく, chicken).

items.q: [野菜]と 牛肉で 料理を 作ります。
items.explanation: 野菜 (vegetables) is read as yasai. 野 (N4.5, field/plains) on-reading や + 菜 (N4.5, vegetable/greens) reading さい = yasai. 野菜 refers to vegetables as a category.

items.q: この [料理]は とても おいしいですよ。
items.explanation: 料理 (cooking / dish) is read as ryouri. 料 (N4.5, materials/fee) on-reading りょう + 理 (N4.5, reason/logic) on-reading り = ryouri. 料理 means a cooked dish or the act of cooking.

items.q: この [物語]が 大好きです。
items.explanation: 物語 (story / tale) is read as monogatari. 物 (N4.5, thing) reads もの + 語 (N5.12, word/language) reads がたり (compound sound change). 物語 literally means 'telling of things' — a story or tale.

items.q: 今日は [鳥肉]の 料理が 食べたいです。
items.explanation: 鳥肉 (chicken meat) is read as toriniku. 鳥 (N4.6, bird) on-reading ちょう / kun-reading とり + 肉 (N4.5, meat) = とりにく. 鳥 alone means a bird; combined with 肉 it specifically refers to chicken as a food ingredient.

items.q: この 料理の [味]は どうですか。
items.explanation: 味 (taste / flavor) is read as aji in its kun-reading. The on-reading み appears in compounds like 調味 (seasoning). Alone, 味 refers to the flavor of food. 味 (N4.6).

items.q: [昼ご飯]を 食べましょう。
items.explanation: 昼ご飯 (lunch) is read as hirugohan. 昼 (N4.6, noon) + ご飯 (N4.6, rice/meal). Contrast: 朝ご飯 (あさごはん, breakfast) and 晩ご飯 (ばんごはん, dinner). 昼休み (ひるやすみ, lunch break) is a common distractor.

items.q: 今朝は [暗い]ですね。
items.explanation: 暗い (dark) is read as kurai. 暗 (N4.6, dark/gloomy) + い (い-adjective marker). Contrast with 明るい (あかるい, bright) from N4.3. Note: くらい as a standalone hiragana word means 'about/approximately' — a different word.

sections.title: Part 5: Context & Vocabulary
sections.instructions: Select the best word to complete the sentence.

items.q: 昼休みに、友だちと ______を 食べました。
items.explanation: 昼ご飯 (lunch) fits: eating lunch during the lunch break with a friend is natural. 晩ご飯 is dinner — an evening meal, not appropriate for a midday break. 飲み物 is a drink — you drink (飲む) it, not eat (食べる) it. 買い物 is shopping — you do (する) shopping, you don't eat it.

items.q: この 料理には 野菜と ______が 入っています。
items.explanation: 牛肉 (beef) fits: beef and vegetables going into a dish is a natural cooking combination. 買い物 is shopping (an activity, not an ingredient). 昼休み is the lunch break (a time period). 物語 is a story (nothing to do with cooking). 入っています = 'is contained in / is included in' (te-form progressive).

items.q: この 料理は ______が とても いいですよ。おいしいです。
items.explanation: 味 (taste/flavor) fits: a dish with very good flavor is delicious. 買い物 is shopping. 物語 is a story. 飲み物 is a drink — but the sentence is about 料理 (a cooked dish), not a beverage. 味がいい = 'has a good taste.'

items.q: ______で、野菜と 牛肉と 飲み物を 買いました。
items.explanation: スーパー (supermarket) fits: buying vegetables, beef, and drinks at a supermarket is natural everyday shopping. レストラン is a restaurant where you eat — not where you buy raw ingredients. 会社 is a company/office. 昼休み is the lunch break — a time period, not a place.

sections.title: Part 6: Grammar Check
sections.instructions: Choose the correct particle or verb form to complete the sentence.

items.q: 鳥肉は 牛肉 ______ おいしいですよ。
items.explanation: Use より (comparison particle, N4.5) to mark the lesser item in a comparison: 牛肉より = 'more than beef.' The pattern 'A は B より adjective' states A is more adjective than B. まで means 'until/as far as.' から after a noun means 'from.' ごろ means 'around/approximately' (used for approximate times).

items.q: 友だちと 昼休みに いっしょに 食べに ______。
items.explanation: 行きましょう (let's go — polite volitional form of 行く) fits: the speaker is extending an invitation to go together. 行きたいです means 'I want to go' — personal desire, not an invitation to another person. 行きます is plain present/future 'will go.' 行きました is past 'went.' いっしょに食べに行きましょう = 'Let's go eat together.'

items.q: この 食べ物と その 飲み物は ______ 味ですか。
items.explanation: 同じ is unique among na-adjectives: it directly precedes nouns WITHOUT な. 同じ味 (the same taste) — NOT 同じな味. Most na-adjectives require な before a noun (e.g. 好きな料理, 大切なもの), but 同じ is the main exception. 同じの makes it possessive ('of the same kind'). 同じく is the adverbial form (similarly/likewise).

items.q: この 料理には 牛肉 ______ 野菜が 入っています。
items.explanation: Use と (connective particle) to list 牛肉 and 野菜 as a pair: 'beef and vegetables.' と links two or more nouns as a complete list (A と B = A and B). が marks a subject — 牛肉が alone would make only beef the subject. を marks a direct object. に marks direction or location. The full pattern: 料理には〜が入っています = 'the dish contains ~.'
